ACCESS_TOKEN.name: GitHub PAT
ACCESS_TOKEN.description: Fine-grained PAT with 'Administration: Read and write' on the repository. The runner uses it once at boot to fetch a registration token; not stored on GitHub.

DOCKER_HUB_USERNAME.name: Docker Hub Username
DOCKER_HUB_USERNAME.description: Docker Hub username for authenticated pulls (200 pulls/6h instead of 100). Leave empty to run anonymously.

LABELS.name: Runner Labels
LABELS.description: Comma-separated labels used in workflow `runs-on:` selectors.

REPO_URL.name: GitHub Repository URL
REPO_URL.description: Full URL of the GitHub repository the runner registers against (e.g. https://github.com/proxvex/proxvex).

RUNNER_NAME.name: Runner Name
RUNNER_NAME.description: Display name shown in GitHub Settings → Actions → Runners.

SMTP_HOST.name: SMTP Host
SMTP_HOST.description: SMTP server hostname (e.g. smtp.mailbox.org). Leave empty to skip SMTP setup.

SMTP_PORT.name: SMTP Port
SMTP_PORT.description: SMTP server port (587 for STARTTLS, 465 for implicit TLS).

SMTP_SENDER.name: SMTP Sender
SMTP_SENDER.description: From address on outgoing mail (e.g. admin@example.com). Must be authorized at the provider.

ZITADEL_EXTERNALDOMAIN.name: Zitadel External Domain
ZITADEL_EXTERNALDOMAIN.description: Public FQDN Zitadel is exposed under. When set, used as Host header and issuer-URL base instead of hostname+domain_suffix.

ZITADEL_EXTERNALPORT.name: External Port
ZITADEL_EXTERNALPORT.description: Public TCP port Zitadel is reached on (e.g. 443 behind nginx, 8080 for direct exposure).

ZITADEL_EXTERNALSECURE.name: External Secure
ZITADEL_EXTERNALSECURE.description: Set to 'true' when Zitadel is reached via HTTPS from outside (e.g. behind a TLS reverse proxy).

ZITADEL_FORWARDED_PROTO.name: Forwarded Proto
ZITADEL_FORWARDED_PROTO.description: X-Forwarded-Proto value the login UI sends to zitadel-api (http or https). Match the public scheme.

ZITADEL_PAT.name: Zitadel PAT
ZITADEL_PAT.description: Short-lived Zitadel access token (injected by backend in zero-secret mode). Falls back to admin-client.pat file if not set.

ZITADEL_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.name: Public Base URL
ZITADEL_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.description: Full public base URL the user types in their browser (no trailing slash). Used in BASEURI/OIDC default URLs. Example: https://auth.example.com (no port for default 443/80).

acme.cert_dir.name: Certificate Directory
acme.cert_dir.description: Container directory where certificates are written

acme.needs_ca_cert.name: Needs CA Certificate
acme.needs_ca_cert.description: Whether to write the CA/intermediate certificate (chain.pem)

acme.needs_server_cert.name: Needs Server Certificate
acme.needs_server_cert.description: Whether to issue a server certificate (privkey, cert, fullchain)

acme_domain.name: Domain
acme_domain.description: Fully qualified domain name for the certificate

acme_email.name: Email
acme_email.description: Email address for Let's Encrypt registration and expiry notifications.

acme_san.name: SAN (Subject Alternative Names)
acme_san.description: Domain(s) for the certificate. Comma-separated for multi-domain SAN (e.g. app.example.com or auth.example.com,api.example.com). Supports wildcards (*.example.com).

acme_staging.name: Staging Mode
acme_staging.description: Use Let's Encrypt staging server (for testing, no rate limits).

addon_content.name: Samba Configuration File
addon_content.description: Base64 encoded content of the addon configuration file

addon_packages.name: Addon Packages
addon_packages.description: Additional packages from addons (merged with base packages).

addon_path.name: Addon File Path
addon_path.description: Target path in format {volume_key}:{filename}. Example: samba_config:smb.conf

addon_volumes.name: Addon Volumes
addon_volumes.description: Additional volumes from addons (merged with base volumes).

all_stack_ids.name: All Stack IDs
all_stack_ids.description: Auto-injected by backend: JSON array of all selected stack IDs, written as separate :stack-id markers

alpine_mirror.name: Alpine Mirror
alpine_mirror.description: Alpine Linux APK mirror URL (e.g., 'http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine'). If empty, default repositories will be used. Should include base URL without version/repository path.

api_login_password.name: API Login Password
api_login_password.description: Password for shared api_login role (PostgREST). Resolved from .env if available.

app_dependencies.name: Application Dependencies
app_dependencies.description: Auto-injected by backend: JSON array of application dependencies

app_name.name: Application Name
app_name.description: Name of the application (used for schema/role naming: <app>_data, <app>_app, etc.)

application_id.name: Application ID
application_id.description: Application identifier for matching the Docker image

arch.name: Architecture
arch.description: Optional: CPU architecture for pct create (amd64, arm64, armhf, i386). Only used for OCI images. For regular LXC templates, Proxmox auto-detects the architecture.

audio_card.name: Audio Card
audio_card.description: Audio card identifier in format card0, card1, etc. (e.g., card0 for first audio card)

bind_privileged_port.name: Bind Privileged Port
bind_privileged_port.description: Allow service to bind to privileged ports (e.g., 80, 443) by setting CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE capability

bootstrap_zitadel.name: Bootstrap Zitadel
bootstrap_zitadel.description: Set to 'true' to run Zitadel bootstrap (creates project, service account, invalidates admin PAT)

cert_dir_override.name: Cert Dir Override
cert_dir_override.description: If set, use this path directly as cert directory instead of computing from shared_volpath

check_file_paths.name: File Paths to Check
check_file_paths.description: Newline-separated list of file paths to verify inside the container.

check_oidc_api_protected.name: Check OIDC API Protected
check_oidc_api_protected.description: Set to true to verify the deployer API requires authentication.

check_oidc_enabled.name: Check OIDC Enabled
check_oidc_enabled.description: Set to true to verify OIDC is enabled on this deployer instance.

check_oidc_machine_login.name: Check OIDC Machine Login
check_oidc_machine_login.description: Set to true to verify end-to-end OIDC machine login.

check_tls_port.name: TLS Port to Check
check_tls_port.description: Port number to verify TLS connection on.

command.name: Command
command.description: The command to run (also used as service and user name)

command_args.name: Command Line Arguments
command_args.description: Arguments for the command. Available variables: $HOME_DIR, $DATA_DIR, $SECURE_DIR

compose_file.name: Docker Compose File
compose_file.description: Docker Compose YAML file (upload). Upload this file first to extract service information.

compose_key.name: Env Key in docker-compose.yml
compose_key.description: The environment key to set (e.g. CF_API_TOKEN).

compose_project.name: Compose Project
compose_project.description: Project name for Docker Compose (used for named volumes and directory). Defaults to hostname if not provided.

container_device_paths.name: Container Device Paths
container_device_paths.description: Optional: Comma-separated container device paths (default: auto-detect based on card number). Note: This parameter is currently not used, devices are mapped to standard /dev/snd/* paths.

database.name: Target Database
database.description: Database to create schemas in (default: postgres)

database_name.name: Database Name
database_name.description: Name of the PostgreSQL database to create

debian_mirror.name: Debian Mirror
debian_mirror.description: Debian/Ubuntu APT mirror URL (e.g., 'http://deb.debian.org/debian'). If empty, default repositories will be used.

dep_zitadel_vm_id.name: Zitadel VM ID
dep_zitadel_vm_id.description: VM ID of the Zitadel dependency container.

deployer_base_url.name: Deployer Base URL
deployer_base_url.description: Auto-injected by backend: External deployer URL (e.g. https://deployer.example.com). Used for redirect when set.

disk_size.name: Disk size
disk_size.description: Rootfs size for the container in GiB. Apps that bundle a large image (Node.js, JDK, full Debian base) should override this in their application.json. Default fits Alpine + small OCI images (~200 MiB used).

domain_suffix.name: Domain Suffix
domain_suffix.description: Global DNS domain suffix — provided by 150-conf-setup-oidc-client.

env_file.name: Environment File (.env)
env_file.description: Environment variables file (.env) template (upload, optional). If it contains {{ markers }}, users must upload a real .env at deployment time.

occurrences.description:
Environment variables in key=value format, one per line. Each line sets an environment variable in the container configuration. Example:
MYAPP_HOST=localhost
MYAPP_PORT=8080
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb
occurrences.default:
GID=1000
GITEA_DB_NAME=gitea
GITEA_DB_TYPE=sqlite3
GITEA_DOMAIN=localhost
GITEA_HTTP_PORT=3000
GITEA_ROOT_URL=http://localhost:3000
GITEA_SSH_PORT=2222
GITEA_VERSION=latest
GITEA__database__DB_TYPE=sqlite3
GITEA__database__HOST={{ POSTGRES_HOST }}:5432
GITEA__database__NAME=gitea
GITEA__database__PASSWD={{ POSTGRES_PASSWORD }}
GITEA__database__USER=postgres
GITEA__server__DOMAIN=localhost
GITEA__server__HTTP_PORT=3000
GITEA__server__ROOT_URL=http://localhost:3000
GITEA__server__SSH_DOMAIN=localhost
GITEA__server__SSH_PORT=2222
POSTGRES_USER=postgres
UID=1000
USER_GID=1000
USER_UID=1000

occurrences.description: Environment variables to substitute in initial_command.

flow_password.name: Credential Password
flow_password.description: Password for credential nodes (e.g. database password)

flow_source.name: Flow Source
flow_source.description: URL or path to the flow JSON file to import

flow_username.name: Credential Username
flow_username.description: Username for credential nodes (e.g. database user)

occurrences.description: Optional: GID of the group in the container who should have access to the input device (LXC only). Default is 0 (root). For VMs, this parameter is ignored.
Group ID for file ownership
Group ID for file ownership
Group ID for file ownership

group.name: Group Name
group.description: Optional group name (will be created if not exists)

host_device_path.name: USB Serial Port
host_device_path.description: USB serial port device path on the host

occurrences.description: Container hostname. Used as compose_project default when none was given. Auto-resolved from container config on reconfigure.
Hostname for the LXC container
Container hostname
Container hostname
Container hostname

http_port.name: HTTP Port
http_port.description: Port number for PostgREST HTTP server (default: 3000)

https_port.name: HTTPS Port
https_port.description: The HTTPS port to listen on (default: 1443, rootless containers cannot bind 443).

icon_base64.name: Icon Base64
icon_base64.description: Auto-injected by backend: Base64-encoded application icon

icon_mime_type.name: Icon MIME Type
icon_mime_type.description: Auto-injected by backend: Icon MIME type (image/svg+xml or image/png)

initial_command.name: Initial Command
initial_command.description: Initial command to execute in the container (lxc.init_cmd). Useful for OCI images that require a specific start command.

install_replug_watcher.name: Live Replug (Host Installation)
install_replug_watcher.description: Install a watcher service on the host to handle device replug events

is_deployer.name: Is Deployer
is_deployer.description: Auto-injected by backend: Set to 'true' when installing proxvex itself

lxc_var_name.name: Environment Variable Name
lxc_var_name.description: Name of the env variable (e.g. POSTGRES_PASSWORD). Becomes lxc.environment.<NAME>=<VAL>.

occurrences.description: Optional: The mapped GID on the host (output from setup-lxc-uid-mapping.py in template 102). If not provided, the script will calculate it. Use this when using 1:1 UID mapping instead of the standard offset.
Mapped group ID for unprivileged containers
Mapped group ID for unprivileged containers
Mapped group ID for unprivileged containers

Optional: The mapped UID on the host (output from setup-lxc-uid-mapping.py in template 102). If not provided, the script will calculate it. Use this when using 1:1 UID mapping instead of the standard offset.
Mapped user ID for unprivileged containers
Mapped user ID for unprivileged containers
Mapped user ID for unprivileged containers

memory.name: Memory in MB
memory.description: Memory for the container in MB

nameserver6.name: nameserver6
nameserver6.description: IPv6 DNS nameserver (e.g. fd00:...::1 or 2001:...::1). Optional.

new_value.name: New Value
new_value.description: New value for the shell variable.

oci_image.name: OCI Image
oci_image.description: Optional: OCI image reference used to create the container (e.g. docker://mariadb:11.0.2). If provided, it will be written into the Proxmox notes (description) as an identifiable line.

oci_image_tag.name: Version
oci_image_tag.description: OCI image tag / version to pull (e.g. '0.5.28', 'v4.12.3'). Explicit versions keep registry-mirror caches deterministic and make tests reproducible. Apps typically template this into their `oci_image` property (e.g. `ghcr.io/foo/bar:{{oci_image_tag}}`). Reconfigure does not execute this template, so the reconfigure path inherits the tag already stored in the container's notes.

oidc_app_name.name: OIDC Application Name
oidc_app_name.description: Name of the OIDC application in Zitadel (defaults to hostname). Used when the deployer can talk to the Zitadel Management API to auto-create or look up the client.

oidc_callback_path.name: Callback Path
oidc_callback_path.description: Path the OIDC provider redirects to after login (from app.properties).

oidc_client_id.name: OIDC Client ID
oidc_client_id.description: Existing OIDC client ID from Zitadel. Provide together with Client Secret and Issuer URL when auto-provisioning via the Zitadel Management API is not available (e.g. deployer OIDC login is disabled or pointing at an external Zitadel).

oidc_client_secret.name: OIDC Client Secret
oidc_client_secret.description: OIDC client secret for client_credentials fallback (provided by addon-oidc).

oidc_issuer_url.name: OIDC Issuer URL
oidc_issuer_url.description: OIDC issuer URL (e.g. https://zitadel.example.com). Required together with Client ID and Secret for the manual entry mode.

oidc_project_name.name: Zitadel Project Name
oidc_project_name.description: Name of the Zitadel project for this application (defaults to hostname). Used for auto-provisioning via the Zitadel Management API.

oidc_required_role.name: OIDC Required Role
oidc_required_role.description: Zitadel project role required for access (from app.properties). If empty, any authenticated user is allowed.

oidc_roles.name: OIDC Roles
oidc_roles.description: JSON array of role definitions from application.json (injected by backend)

ostype.name: OS Type
ostype.description: Operating system type: 'alpine' for Alpine Linux (apk) or 'debian'/'ubuntu' for Debian/Ubuntu (apt).

owned_paths.name: Owned Paths
owned_paths.description: Space-separated list of files/directories the user should own with rw access

package.name: Package name
package.description: Name of the npm package to install

packagerpubkeyurl.name: URL of the public key for verifying the package
packagerpubkeyurl.description: URL of the public key for verifying the package

packages.name: Packages
packages.description: Space-separated list of packages to install. For Alpine Linux, use APK package names. For Debian/Ubuntu, use apt package names.

packageurl.name: URL of the APK package
packageurl.description: URL of the APK package to be installed

platform.name: Platform
platform.description: Target platform (e.g., linux/amd64, linux/arm64). Auto-detects if not specified.

previous_vm_id.name: Previous VM ID
previous_vm_id.description: Previous container VMID (upgrade/reconfigure). Used to prefer reusing that CT's existing volumes over any other historical match.

registry_password.name: Registry Password
registry_password.description: Password for registry authentication (optional, required for private images)

registry_token.name: Registry Token
registry_token.description: Bearer token for registry authentication (optional, alternative to username/password)

registry_username.name: Registry Username
registry_username.description: Username for registry authentication (optional, required for private images)

rest_content_type.name: REST Content-Type
rest_content_type.description: Content-Type header sent with the POST.

rest_health_path.name: Health Endpoint Path
rest_health_path.description: Path polled until the application reports readiness.

rest_path.name: REST Endpoint Path
rest_path.description: Path of the local-settings import endpoint.

rest_payload_b64.name: REST Payload (base64)
rest_payload_b64.description: Base64-encoded ZIP payload. Defaults to the uploaded ZIP content.

rest_url.name: REST Base URL
rest_url.description: Base URL of the application REST API (relative to the container).

rootfs_storage.name: Rootfs Storage
rootfs_storage.description: Proxmox storage for the container rootfs

script_var.name: Shell Variable Name
script_var.description: Shell variable whose value is patched (e.g. CF_API_TOKEN).

searchdomain.name: DNS Search Domain
searchdomain.description: DNS search domain for the container's resolv.conf. Default (empty) avoids inheriting the PVE host's hostname domain (e.g. 'cluster' when the host is 'pve1.cluster'), which can break bare-hostname DNS resolution from inside docker containers (Go's resolver and dnsmasq embedded DNS append the search suffix and don't fall back to the bare name). Set explicitly only if you need a specific search domain.

server_cert_b64.name: Server Cert
server_cert_b64.description: Base64-encoded server certificate PEM (signed by backend)

server_key_b64.name: Server Key
server_key_b64.description: Base64-encoded server private key PEM (signed by backend)

occurrences.description: Deployer-global shared volume path on the PVE host — used by app scripts that edit files relative to the LXC volume (e.g. Node-RED settings.js).
Path to the shared volume mount point
Path to the shared volume mount point
Path to the shared volume mount point

smtp_mail_domain.name: Mail domain
smtp_mail_domain.description: Apex domain the mail is sent from (e.g. example.com). Looked up in Cloudflare and used as the zone for MX/SPF records.

smtp_mx_target.name: MX target
smtp_mx_target.description: Hostname the MX record points at (e.g. mxext1.mailbox.org). Consult your mail provider's documentation.

smtp_own_domain.name: Own mail domain (configure Cloudflare DNS)
smtp_own_domain.description: When checked, create MX and SPF DNS records in Cloudflare for the mail domain (requires a cloudflare stack with CF_TOKEN). Leave unchecked if using an existing hosted mail setup where DNS already points to the provider.

smtp_spf_value.name: SPF TXT value
smtp_spf_value.description: Full SPF TXT record value, e.g. 'v=spf1 include:mailbox.org ~all'. Consult your mail provider's documentation.

ssl.needs_server_cert.name: Needs Server Cert
ssl.needs_server_cert.description: Write server certificate files (privkey.pem, cert.pem, fullchain.pem)

ssl_mode.name: SSL Mode
ssl_mode.description: SSL mode: proxy (nginx reverse proxy), native (app handles HTTPS), or certs (certificates only).

stack_id.name: Stack ID
stack_id.description: Auto-injected by backend: Primary stack ID for dependency discovery

stack_secret_names.name: Stack Secret Names
stack_secret_names.description: Comma-separated NAME=VALUE pairs for secrets to sanitize in template file

startup_down.name: Startup Down Delay
startup_down.description: Optional: Seconds to wait after shutting down this container before stopping the next one. Maps to Proxmox --startup down=N.

startup_order.name: Startup Order
startup_order.description: Optional: Boot order for the container. Lower values start first (e.g. 10 for databases, 20 for auth providers). Default 100 for normal apps. Maps to Proxmox --startup order=N.

startup_timeout.name: Startup Timeout
startup_timeout.description: Seconds to wait for Docker services to become healthy (--wait-timeout).

startup_up.name: Startup Up Delay
startup_up.description: Optional: Seconds to wait after starting this container before starting the next one. Maps to Proxmox --startup up=N.

static_gw6.name: static_gw6
static_gw6.description: Static IPv6 gateway (e.g. fd00::1). Requires static_ip6.

static_ip.name: static_ip
static_ip.description: Static IPv4 address in CIDR notation (e.g. 192.168.4.100/24).

static_ip6.name: static_ip6
static_ip6.description: Static IPv6 address in CIDR notation (e.g. fd00::50/64).

storage.name: Storage
storage.description: Proxmox storage where the OCI image should be stored

storage_selection.name: Storage Selection
storage_selection.description: Select storage to mount. Only filesystems prefixed with 'uuid:' are supported.

target_versions.name: Target Versions
target_versions.description: Comma-separated service=version pairs. For OCI-image apps: main=version to pull a specific tag.

template_path.name: Path to the LXC template
template_path.description: Path to the LXC template (e.g. local:vztmpl/alpine-3.19-default_20231107_amd64.tar.xz). Required for new installations (auto-selected for Alpine). Not needed in reconfiguration mode when vm_id points to an existing container.

occurrences.description: Optional: UID of the user in the container who should have access to the input device (LXC only). Default is 0 (root). For VMs, this parameter is ignored.
User ID for file ownership
User ID for file ownership
User ID for file ownership

upload_flows_json_content.name: flows.json
upload_flows_json_content.description: Optional Node-RED flows file (flows.json). When omitted, Node-RED starts with empty flows.

upload_modbus2mqtt_config_content.name: modbus2mqtt local settings ZIP
upload_modbus2mqtt_config_content.description: ZIP file produced by 'Download Local Settings' (https://<host>:<port>/specifications). Contains busses, slaves and local specifications. secrets.yaml is intentionally excluded — set MQTT credentials via the UI after first boot.

upload_mosquitto_configuration_content.name: Mosquitto Configuration
upload_mosquitto_configuration_content.description:
Upload your mosquitto.conf file.

If the optional addon "SSL/HTTPS" is enabled, TLS certificates are automatically provisioned at:
- `/mosquitto/config/certs/cert.pem` (server cert)
- `/mosquitto/config/certs/privkey.pem` (private key)
- `/mosquitto/config/certs/chain.pem` (CA cert)
- `/mosquitto/config/certs/fullchain.pem` (server cert + CA)

MQTTS listener example:
```
listener 8883
certfile /mosquitto/config/certs/cert.pem
cafile /mosquitto/config/certs/chain.pem
keyfile /mosquitto/config/certs/privkey.pem
```
See [Mosquitto TLS docs](https://mosquitto.org/man/mosquitto-conf-5.html)

usb_bus_device.name: USB Bus:Device
usb_bus_device.description: USB bus and device number in format bus:device (e.g., 1:3 for /dev/bus/usb/001/003)

username.name: Username
username.description: Optional: Username to create on the VE host before container creation. This ensures consistent UID/GID mapping between host and container. Default is root. If not provided, root will be used.

ve_context_key.name: VE Context Key
ve_context_key.description: Auto-injected by backend: VE context identifier for API calls.

ve_debug_commands.name: VE Debug Commands
ve_debug_commands.description: Comma-separated list of command name substrings to debug-dump during execution. For each matching command the backend emits the current inputs+defaults (before) and outputs (after) to stderr of the deploy log. Use '*' to dump every command. Leave empty to disable. Example: 'Write Docker Compose Notes,acme-renew'

version.name: Version
version.description: Version of the package to install

vm_id.name: LXC Container ID
vm_id.description: VM ID — used to look up the running container's actual hostname (the one volumes are named after, which can differ from the input hostname during reconfigure).

vm_id_start.name: VM ID Start
vm_id_start.description: Optional: Start index for auto-assigned VM IDs. The next free ID starting from this value will be used.

volume_backup.name: Include in Backup
volume_backup.description: Whether the volume should be included in Proxmox backups

volume_size.name: Volume Size
volume_size.description: Default size per managed mp volume (e.g., 4M, 256M, 1G, 2.5G). Most volumes hold KB of state (config, secrets, certs, bootstrap PATs); the default fits those. Per-volume override is supported by appending `,size=…` to a line in the `volumes` property: e.g. `data=/var/lib/gitea,size=2G`.

volume_storage.name: Volume Storage
volume_storage.description: Proxmox storage ID for data volumes. Auto-detected from rootfs if not set.

occurrences.description: Existing volume definitions with options (e.g. permissions). Merged with extracted volumes.
One per line: key=/container/path

wait_for_network.name: Wait for Network
wait_for_network.description: Wrap entrypoint to wait for network before starting. Only needed for apps that connect to external services at startup.

with_postgrest.name: Create PostgREST Roles
with_postgrest.description: Create additional API schema and roles for PostgREST (api_login, <app>_anon, <app>_user)

zitadel_project_id.name: Zitadel Project ID
zitadel_project_id.description: Output from bootstrap step (presence triggers hardening)
